description_long: This kiosk complex consists of four interconnected wooden structures elevated on stilts, each featuring large, rectangular windows and gabled shingle roofs. The buildings are connected by wooden walkways and stairs, creating a cohesive, multi-unit layout suitable for commercial or recreational use. The exterior walls are clad in wood, with prominent window panels providing ample natural light. The roofs have a pronounced overhang, offering shade and protection from the elements. The design is open and accessible, with multiple entry points and visible walkways between the units. The overall appearance is rustic yet functional, blending traditional materials with a modular, community-oriented layout.
description: A group of four elevated wooden kiosks with large windows, gabled shingle roofs, and connecting walkways. The design is rustic and modular, featuring open access and prominent overhangs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four kiosks in a linear arrangement, with connecting walkways and stairs clearly visible between the units.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth of the complex, the gabled roofs, and the spatial relationship between the kiosks and walkways.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and the elevated structure of the kiosks on stilts.
description_view_3: Oblique view showing the side profiles of the kiosks, the overhanging roofs, and the interconnected walkways.
attribute_window_presence: Multiple large rectangular windows on all sides of each kiosk unit
attribute_door_configuration: At least four single doors, one per kiosk, located at the walkways or entry points
attribute_roof_design: Gabled roofs with gray asphalt shingles and pronounced overhangs
